user_message: Give me the manager view on this Quantum Analytics support ticket. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Quantum Analytics
Date: 2024-01-02
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), David (Quantum Analytics)

[00:02:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:08:00] David (Quantum Analytics): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:18:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] David (Quantum Analytics): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:26:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:31:00] David (Quantum Analytics): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: solution sketch needed from discovery signals

required_artifacts: BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Discovery Call Transcript -> Opportunity Build template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
